_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of anti-forensics tactics, techniques, and procedures.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of lawful bases for data processing under applicable regulations, including consent requirements, contractual necessity, legal obligations, vital interests, public task authority, and legitimate interest balancing tests.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI RC domain. RC-K-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-K-011 (SP, cosine: 0.401), RM-K-005 (RM, cosine: 0.397).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.5/10 (raw 0.247). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [anti, forensics, procedures, tactics, techniques] vs WIDAI-only terms [applicable, authority, balancing, bases, consent, contractual] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.864 (P=0.900, R=0.832). P exceeds R by 0.068 — the DCWF element's 7 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 27 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.8%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of anti-forensics tactics, techniques, and procedures." and "Knowledge of lawful bases for data processing under applicable regulations, incl". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.412 > STS 0.247 (gap +0.164). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [knowledge] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 9.4%. Shared tokens: [knowledge]. DCWF-only: [anti, forensics, procedures, tactics, techniques]. WIDAI-only: [applicable, authority, balancing, bases, consent, contractual].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.8% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.164 with low STS 0.247. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
